item.text: I found one avoidable dependency: the page pulled Google Fonts even though the rest of the app is fully local. I’m removing that so the app remains self-contained and consistent with the “open the file directly” setup.
The app is now self-contained. I’m doing one final mechanical check on the JavaScript and the entry files, then I’ll summarize the single goal I picked and what changed.

item.aggregated_output:
# Decision Journal

A small static app for recording meaningful decisions, the alternatives considered, and the eventual outcome.

## What it does

- Stores entries in `localStorage`
- Tracks confidence and review dates
- Filters between open, reviewed, and positive-outcome decisions
- Shows lightweight summary stats

## Run it

Open [index.html](/workspace/index.html) directly in a browser.

item.text:
I picked one concrete next goal: turn the empty workspace into a usable product by building a self-contained decision journal.

The implementation is in [index.html](/workspace/index.html), [styles.css](/workspace/styles.css), and [app.js](/workspace/app.js). It gives you a local browser app for recording decisions, alternatives, confidence, and review dates, then updating outcomes later with filtering and summary stats. I also added a short run note in [README.md](/workspace/README.md).

Verification: `node --check /workspace/app.js` passed. I didn’t run a browser-based UI test here, but the app is designed to work by opening [index.html](/workspace/index.html) directly.

I picked one concrete next goal: turn the empty workspace into a usable product by building a self-contained decision journal.

The implementation is in [index.html](/workspace/index.html), [styles.css](/workspace/styles.css), and [app.js](/workspace/app.js). It gives you a local browser app for recording decisions, alternatives, confidence, and review dates, then updating outcomes later with filtering and summary stats. I also added a short run note in [README.md](/workspace/README.md).
